Erin was appointed by the judge to ensure the best interests of my daughter (who was four years old at the time), since both her father and I were represented by attorneys. Prior to court, she confronted me and accused me of coaching my daughter to lie about the traumatic events she experienced. She further stated that the CPS caseworker would support her and confirm these claims. This was despite the fact that two doctors, two nurses, and a therapist had all confirmed my daughter’s account.
When a child is taken to the hospital for an examination, they are separated from the parent and questioned to determine whether the story changes under different circumstances, which helps identify if the child is being coerced. A full medical examination is also performed. In my daughter’s case, both doctors confirmed that the abuse occurred. For Erin to accuse me of fabricating my daughter’s testimony was devastating. At no point did my daughter ever change her story.
When I first met Erin, her initial comment was, “Well, this is a very detailed story for a four-year-old.” I dismissed the remark and gave her the benefit of the doubt, believing she would protect my daughter’s best interests. The only reason she did not present her accusations to the judge was because the CPS caseworker, who was allegedly going to support her claims, failed to appear at the scheduled Zoom hearing. I later reported that CPS caseworker and received a written response confirming that my concerns were valid and that the case had not been thoroughly investigated. Additionally, my attorney contacted my child’s father’s attorney, who admitted he had never spoken with the CPS caseworker and that Erin was the one who relayed the false claim that I had been lying.
Erin’s conduct was deeply unprofessional and harmful, and I strongly caution others against seeking her assistance. She is now running for judge, but someone who has demonstrated a willingness to misrepresent the truth should not be entrusted with such a position of authority.

With Ricardo Barrera at the helm, I experienced a level of professionalism and respect that is often hard to find in legal representation. His approach was not merely transactional; it was deeply humanistic, prioritizing my needs and those of my daughter throughout a grueling two-year contested divorce process. Moreover, the accessibility provided by Mr. Barrera was indispensable during critical moments leading up to our jury trial. Knowing that I could reach out for guidance or reassurance whenever necessary allowed me to focus on what truly mattered: securing a positive outcome for myself and my daughter. This emergency access was not just about legal advice; it instilled in me a sense of security during an inherently uncertain time. Such support proved invaluable as we navigated complex legal waters.

Ultimately, my victory in winning custody with no geographic restrictions marked not only a significant legal achievement but also symbolized the reclamation of our lives post-divorce. reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Five-Star Review – Child Support & Custody Representation

I don’t write reviews lightly, but I have to speak on this man’s work and integrity.

He didn’t just represent me. He fought for me, and he did it with clarity, respect, and full transparency the whole way through. Every step of the process, he gave it to me straight. No games. No sugar-coating. Just the truth about where I stood, what my options were, and what it would take to move forward.

We went in for a child support review hearing, dealing with a situation that had me deep in back pay, tied up in things that were out of my control, and honestly, I didn’t have the legal knowledge or the tools to navigate those waters. But he did. And he never once made me feel less for needing that help.

More than anything, I saw his heart. A man of faith, a family man, and someone who lives what he says. You don’t feel like a case number when he’s representing you. You feel like someone who matters.

He’s also affordable, and I say that with full respect. Yes, legal help costs money—but you’re paying for someone’s time, experience, and ability to step into something you can’t handle alone. And with him, you’re not just paying a fee. You’re investing in someone who truly works with you. He made it possible for me to get the help I needed without putting me in a worse position financially.

His office staff deserves just as much praise. The women who handle the phones, the emails, the payments, the paperwork—they are incredible. Professional, respectful, and grounded. Every time I had to call or check in, I felt like I was dealing with people who actually cared. It’s a well-managed, well-run firm from top to bottom.

When things started shifting last minute, he stayed steady. He adapted. He made sure my voice and truth were heard. This isn’t just some lawyer reading off a case file. This is the one you want in your corner.

By the end of the hearing, he helped deliver the outcome we had all been hoping and praying for.
All glory to God, in Jesus’ name. Amen.
